sitrep_per_zone_display.basis: Reviewed SitRep Table 1 health-zone rows for map markers/shading; the explicit unventilated residual is retained as residual and not allocated to named zones.

source_conflict_notes: Suspected/reported-case count spans 395 (Africa CDC PHECS, 18 May 2026) to 1077 suspected DRC cases (ECDC 27 May 2026, citing 'On 26 May, the Ministry of Health in DRC reported'), the highest valid primary on the latest date. INRB build-2026-05-28 (national_moh, DRC-only restricted GitHub release with data-as-of 26 May) cross-corroborates 1077. The CDC 25 May 906, ECDC 25 May 904, and the DRC MoH all-published-bulletins dashboard aggregate of 854 reported cases (24 May) are retained as dated conflict anchors and not used to down-revise the higher endpoint.

Confirmed deaths reconciled across INRB/INSP/UMIE build-2026-05-28-bb8b7d5 (17 DRC confirmed deaths, national_moh, data-as-of 26 May, DRC-only) and ECDC 27 May (1 Uganda confirmed death) = 18 country-scope confirmed deaths. Suspected deaths reconciled from INRB/INSP/UMIE build-2026-05-28-bb8b7d5 cumul deces suspects (246 DRC). ECDC's 238 suspected DRC deaths on the same data date, CDC 25 May (223), ECDC 25 May (119), Africa CDC PHECS 18 May (106), and the DRC MoH 24 May dashboard aggregate (179) drop to dated conflict anchors. The two series are kept separate per INRB's published schema; the prior cross-class composition that summed suspected and confirmed under one headline is retired.

Confirmed count spans 10 (WHO PHEIC statement, 17 May 2026, case data as of 16 May: 8 Ituri + 2 Kampala; Kinshasa case deconfirmed) to 128 total country-scope confirmed cases (ECDC 27 May 2026, citing DRC MoH 26 May: 121 DRC + 7 Uganda). INRB build-2026-05-28 (DRC-only national_moh) cross-corroborates the DRC component (121). The CDC 25 May 112 (105 DRC + 7 Uganda), ECDC 25 May 101 confirmed, and the DRC MoH 24 May dashboard aggregate of 112 confirmed DRC are retained as dated conflict anchors. CDC and WHO AFRO have not yet published an edition that catches up to the DRC MoH 26 May release.

Spatial model source zones use the reviewed INSP SitRep Table 1 (inrb-sitrep-053-2026-07-06; source published through 2026-07-06), which attributes 1691 confirmed cases across 37 monitored health zones (37 with confirmed cases). The national DRC and country-scope headline confirmed totals are higher; the difference is carried as unallocated and cross-border attribution-lag context rather than smeared across every source zone. Corridor source load therefore uses the reviewed per-health-zone confirmed vector.

CDC 24 May reports five Uganda cases, but does not publish a zone-attributed count table. The DRC MoH dashboard exposes all-published-bulletins aggregate cards and sparse SitRep 009 rows; the aggregate is carried as official count evidence, while the latest sparse rows remain source-review and are not promoted to corridor source load until a cumulative PDF/table label is verified. One American national was evacuated from DRC to Germany and confirmed positive; a high-risk contact was reportedly transferred to Czechia. The reported Kinshasa case was deconfirmed by INRB and is not counted as confirmed.

Per-source archive status: all cited sources are registered in data/bundibugyo-2026/manifest.json. WHO DON 602, WHO PHEIC, WHO DG remarks on 20 and 22 May, WHO IHR temporary recommendations, WHO AFRO landing page, CDC HAN, CDC Current Situation, ECDC May 19/21, and the consensus aggregator are byte-archived with SHA-256; DRC MoH dashboard GraphQL bytes, Africa CDC, Imperial, and PAHO/WHO alert PDF are hash-recorded with restricted raw publisher bytes kept private pending terms or permission confirmation.

INRB SitRep #015 (data cutoff 2026-05-29, published 2026-05-30) promoted the DRC headline tiles: cumul cas confirmes 263, cumul deces parmi confirmes 42, cumul cas suspects 349 (the value on the SitRep tile carries a superscript footnote marker, not a trailing digit; the cumulative-suspect count was revised down from a pre-investigation 1077 in SitRep #014 with footnote 'revised downward after investigation confirmed some and ruled out others'), gueris 2. Country-scope confirmed = 263 DRC + 7 UGA (ECDC 27 May, the last source covering Uganda) = 270. Country-scope confirmed deaths = 42 DRC + 1 UGA = 43. SitRep #015 expanded the zone-affected footprint from 16 (May 28 INSP base) to 22 zones nationally; new zones (Aungba, Gety, Lita, Mangala, Kalunguta, Kyondo) are added to affected_zones but not to the locked corridor watchlist. The cumul deces suspects tile was not included in #015; the 246 value from the prior INRB build (data-as-of 26 May) is carried forward with reason source_schema_evolved.

INRB SitRep #016 (data cutoff 2026-05-30, published 2026-05-31) promoted the refined schema: cumul cas confirmes 282 (DRC, with footnote donnees en cours d'harmonisation), cas confirmes actifs 238 (= 282 - 42 deaths - 2 cured), cas suspects en cours d'investigation 220, cas suspects en isolement 101, suspected_active total 321. Country-scope confirmed = 282 DRC + 7 UGA (ECDC 27 May anchor) = 289. Country-scope confirmed-active = 238 DRC + 7 UGA = 245. Country-scope confirmed deaths = 42 DRC + 1 UGA = 43. The cumul deces suspects tile remains absent; the 246 May 26 value is carried forward with reason source_schema_evolved.

INRB SitRep #017 (Revised; data cutoff 2026-05-31, published 2026-06-01) promoted: cumul cas confirmes 321 (DRC), cumul deces parmi confirmes 48 (DRC; first movement off 42 since #014), cas suspects en cours d'investigation 116 (down from 220 in #016), cas suspects en isolement 104 (up from 101), so suspected_active falls 321 -> 220 (a genuine surveillance movement, the active queue being worked down under the response, not data loss), gueris 6 (up from 2). Confirmed active is recomputed from the identity 321 - 48 - 6 = 267 DRC because the PDF's printed active cell (238) is a stale carry of the #016 value. Country-scope confirmed = 321 DRC + 7 UGA (ECDC 27 May anchor) = 328; confirmed-active = 267 DRC + 7 UGA presumed-active = 274; confirmed deaths = 48 DRC + 1 UGA = 49. The cumul deces suspects tile remains absent; the 246 May 26 value is carried forward with reason source_schema_evolved and is never an input to Method 2.

INRB/INSP SitRep #019 (data cutoff 2026-06-02, published 2026-06-03) was visually reviewed because parser output was partial. Country-scope confirmed = 363 DRC + 15 Uganda anchor (CDC 2 Jun, refreshed from the carried-forward 7) = 378; country-scope confirmed deaths = 62 DRC + 1 Uganda anchor = 63. visibility.method_caveat: No prior daily snapshot series is supplied to Module C for this release; reporting completeness and latency are prior-based, using the Rosello 2015 BDBV Isiro onset-to-notification distribution as the default. This is a historical prior-outbreak estimate, not a fitted 2026 reporting-delay estimate; Camacho 2015 EBOV-Zaire remains the proxy sensitivity comparator.

active_queue_projection.method_basis: reviewed SitRep lab indicators (accumulated), flat Beta(1,1) positivity applied to the operational active-suspected queue (cases under investigation plus cases in isolation); a sibling diagnostic to C1 reporting-completeness that never feeds it
active_queue_projection.formula: confirmed + active suspected queue * recent lab positivity
limitations: Known-queue yield only: the active suspected queue is a response-system stock, not a denominator for total community infections.
Single reviewed lab window today (SitRep #018); extends to a multi-date band automatically as earlier SitReps gain reviewed lab indicators, with no code change.
active_queue_projection.positivity_basis_note: Positivity is the most recent reviewed lab window (84/249 on 2026-07-06). That date uses the reviewed positivity directly; earlier dated windows carry it back onto each date's reported active-suspected queue (those dates' lab work was not separately reviewed) and are flagged carried_back.

analysis_dependency_audit.clock_basis: SitRep #053 (1728 confirmed, data as of 2026-07-06) is the confirmed-case headline. Confirmed-death endpoint is SitRep #053 (582, data as of 2026-07-06) is the laboratory-confirmed death headline. Suspected-death and under-investigation stocks remain absent unless the current reviewed SitRep explicitly republishes them.

Snapshot-level visibility nowcast. Reporting completeness is delay-only (the gamma reporting-delay CDF); the suspect-queue positivity term carries zero weight (DATA_TERM_WEIGHT = 0.0), so it does not drive the completeness posterior. The cumulative suspected tier was retired 2026-06-02, so no cumulative suspected denominator feeds this surface.
C1 confirmable total retired under the C2 active-queue regime (2026-05-29 onward); the reporting-completeness fraction is kept as an input-only reference, not a current model output.

Confirmed-death endpoint is SitRep #053 (582, data as of 2026-07-06) feeds the confirmed-only death-back-projection grid. The broader suspected-death register is not carried onto the current-cycle surface after the confirmed/suspected death-axis split unless a reviewed source republishes it.
Transmission plausibility uses the current reconciled confirmed headline count.

analysis_dependency_audit.blocked_by: Per-health-zone confirmed attribution uses the reviewed INSP SitRep Table 1 (inrb-sitrep-053-2026-07-06): 37 source zones, of which 37 carry confirmed cases, attributing 1691 confirmed. The remaining 37 confirmed are the unallocated headline + cross-border attribution lag, held in the residual pending a coordinated zone-alias bridge and map-geometry expansion rather than smeared across source zones.

analysis_dependency_audit.clock_basis: Module C2 active-queue lab-yield: a SIBLING diagnostic to the Module C (C1) reporting-completeness nowcast, never an input to it. Recent reviewed-SitRep lab positivity (flat Beta(1,1)) applied to the operational active-suspected queue (cases under investigation plus cases in isolation), added to confirmed. A known-queue yield within the response system, not reporting completeness, hidden community incidence, deaths, or future spread.

calibration_commitments.public_question: Within the 30-day window (registered 2026-07-05, resolving 2026-08-04), will DRC report at least 1 NEW affected health zone beyond the 36 already affected as of the frozen baseline (WHO DON612, data 2026-07-01: Ituri 24, North Kivu 11, South Kivu 1 = 36 national affected health zones)? Resolves on public evidence dated on or after 2026-07-05. This is a relative-high spatial-extent forecast: the geography model ranks continued in-country expansion as more likely than not on an active Ituri-anchored plateau, but the ranking is NOT a calibrated diffusion probability.
calibration_commitments.notes: YES if any WHO DON/SitRep, ECDC CDTR, or INSP/DRC MoH SitRep dated on or after 2026-07-05 reports a count of DRC affected health zones >= 37, OR names a specific health zone as newly affected/confirmed that is not among the 36 in the baseline. NO if the affected-health-zone total remains at 36 or lower through 2026-08-04. Frozen baseline = 36 national affected health zones (Ituri 24 / North Kivu 11 / South Kivu 1). Lower bound for qualifying evidence: 2026-07-05.
calibration_commitments.resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.

calibration_commitments.public_question: Within the 30-day window (registered 2026-07-05, resolving 2026-08-04), will the karisimbi (North Kivu, DRC) health zone reach its FIRST lab-confirmed BDBV case dated on or after 2026-07-05? Resolves ONLY on a first lab-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05, so if karisimbi was already affected before that date this pin resolves NO. This is a relative-mid corridor-watch forecast reflecting a mid-ranked North Kivu edge, NOT a calibrated arrival probability.
calibration_commitments.registration_baseline: no_confirmation_before_2026-07-05
calibration_commitments.notes: YES only if a WHO DON/SitRep, ECDC CDTR, or INSP/DRC MoH SitRep dated on or after 2026-07-05 records the FIRST laboratory-confirmed BDBV case in karisimbi health zone with that first confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05. NO if karisimbi records no first confirmation in-window, OR if its first confirmation predates 2026-07-05 (already-affected zones resolve NO). Lower bound for qualifying evidence: 2026-07-05.
calibration_commitments.resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.

calibration_commitments.public_question: Within the 30-day window (registered 2026-07-05, resolving 2026-08-04), will the mahagi (Ituri, DRC) health zone, our named #1 corridor blindspot on the Mahagi/Goli-Arua axis, reach its FIRST lab-confirmed BDBV case dated on or after 2026-07-05? Resolves ONLY on a first lab-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05, so if mahagi was already affected before that date this pin resolves NO. This is a relative-mid corridor-watch forecast for a specifically pre-registered blindspot, NOT a calibrated arrival probability.
calibration_commitments.registration_baseline: no_confirmation_before_2026-07-05
calibration_commitments.notes: YES only if a WHO DON/SitRep, ECDC CDTR, or INSP/DRC MoH SitRep dated on or after 2026-07-05 records the FIRST laboratory-confirmed BDBV case in mahagi health zone with that first confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05. NO if mahagi records no first confirmation in-window, OR if its first confirmation predates 2026-07-05. Lower bound for qualifying evidence: 2026-07-05.
calibration_commitments.resolution_source_policy: Resolve from public MOH, WHO, Africa CDC, CDC, ECDC, INRB, or other cited public authority reporting dated on or after the 2026-07-05 registration and available by the resolution date; unresolved or ambiguous public evidence remains open until documented review.

calibration_commitments.public_question: Within the 30-day window (registered 2026-07-05, resolving 2026-08-04), CONDITIONAL on at least one of {karisimbi, mahagi} AND kisangani (Tshopo, DRC) both reaching a first BDBV lab-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05, will a nearer Ituri/North-Kivu edge zone (karisimbi OR mahagi) reach that first confirmation on a date at or before kisangani's first confirmation? Resolves ONLY on first confirmations dated on or after 2026-07-05. This is a relative-high pairwise ordinal forecast: the geography model ranks the nearer edge ahead of the farther Tshopo capital, and ordinal rank is the calibrated-ranking claim (plain-distance within-anchor AUC ~0.68), NOT a diffusion probability.
calibration_commitments.registration_baseline: nearer_edge_precedes_kisangani
calibration_commitments.notes: Scoreable ONLY if BOTH a nearer edge (karisimbi or mahagi) AND kisangani each register a FIRST lab-confirmation dated on or after 2026-07-05 within the window. YES if the earliest qualifying nearer-edge first-confirmation date is <= kisangani's first-confirmation date. NO if kisangani confirms strictly before every qualifying nearer-edge zone. If the antecedent is not met (kisangani never confirms in-window, or no nearer edge confirms in-window), the pairwise ordinal is not evaluable and resolves per the ledger's not-evaluable handling, not YES/NO. Any confirmation predating 2026-07-05 does not count. Lower bound for qualifying evidence: 2026-07-05.

calibration_commitments.public_question: As of registration (2026-07-05), the ratio of new confirmed cases to samples tested (lab positivity) in the DRC BDBV response is running in the high-teens-to-low-30s percent (SitRep-47, 2026-06-30: lab positivity 29.9%, samples tested 244 that period; recent prior periods 17.7%-18.4%) while per-period samples tested has RISEN materially from the ~35-70 range of late May to the 140-265 range of late June. FORECAST (YES = plateau, not hidden growth): across public SitReps dated on or after 2026-07-05, lab positivity (new confirmed / samples tested) FALLS or stays roughly FLAT while samples tested per period holds or rises, i.e. the response is widening the net rather than a rising positivity revealing hidden growth. Resolves NO if positivity rises materially and sustainedly WHILE testing also rises (the hidden-growth signature).
calibration_commitments.registration_baseline: SitRep-47 (2026-06-30): lab_positivity_rate 29.9%, samples_tested 244/period; late-June testing 141-265/period vs late-May 35-70/period
calibration_commitments.notes: Resolution source: INSP RDC / WHO AFRO SitRep operational-indicator series (lab_positivity_rate and samples_tested) dated on or after 2026-07-05. YES = over the on/after-7/5 SitReps, mean lab positivity does not rise materially above the ~29.9% 7/5 anchor while mean samples-tested-per-period holds at or above the ~200 late-June level (positivity flat/down as testing holds/rises = widening net). NO = positivity climbs materially and is sustained while testing also climbs (hidden-growth signature). NEVER infer incidence growth from alert or case volume alone. Lower bound: only SitReps dated on or after 2026-07-05 count.

calibration_commitments_meta.source: Sanitized public accountability extract (public_calibration_commitments.json); model internals excluded.

revision_note: Snapshot is published 2026-05-28 over the 26 May data date and supersedes the 26 May snapshot (forward-dated method evolution under painting/immutability, not an in-place re-cut). The new surveillance inputs are the ECDC 27 May page (citing 'On 26 May, the Ministry of Health in DRC reported a total of 121 confirmed cases (including 17 deaths) and 1 077 suspected cases (including 238 deaths) in Ituri, North Kivu, and South Kivu provinces. Uganda has reported seven confirmed cases, including one death.') and the INRB build-2026-05-28 GitHub release (DRC-only national_moh, data-as-of 26 May), both byte-archived/hash-recorded. The promoted endpoints are 128 total confirmed (121 DRC + 7 Uganda) and 18 country-scope confirmed deaths (17 DRC from INRB/INSP + one Uganda from ECDC/CDC). Laboratory-confirmed cases and confirmed deaths are the only cumulative epidemiological metrics; the legacy cross-class composite that summed 246 DRC suspected deaths with one Uganda confirmed death into a single 247 country-scope deaths headline is retired (it conflated confirmed deaths with under-investigation suspected deaths). The CDC 25 May page (112 confirmed, 223 suspected deaths) and the ECDC 25 May page (101 confirmed, 119 suspected deaths) drop to dated conflict anchors. CDC and WHO AFRO have not yet published an edition that catches up to the DRC MoH 26 May release. ECDC's 238 DRC suspected-death figure is retained as a same-day conflict anchor below INRB/INSP's 246. SitRep 009 and the DRC MoH dashboard rows remain source-review (no dateRapportage, no official PDF at capture). The corridor source-load is re-based (2026-05-28, forward-only) onto the INRB-UMIE/INSP per-health-zone series (by_lovs_zone): 18 monitored health zones carrying 109 zone-attributed confirmed cases (11 with confirmed cases), superseding the earlier SitRep-007 CDC/INSP hybrid. The unified confirmed cascade is 128 country-scope -> 121 DRC INSP-attributable national -> 109 zone-attributed + 12 DRC unallocated residual; the remaining 128-121=7 is the cross-border attribution lag (Uganda cases not in the DRC national series). Candidate target zones include arua-uga and nebbi-uga to close the documented Mahagi/Goli<->Arua cross-border blindspot. Pre-committed calibration points are carried forward UNCHANGED from data/calibration-ledger.json; no pin was re-derived. Mobility and confirmation-latency leverages are held as situational inputs (run_local) and are not injected into this provenance-strict public snapshot. See data/external_sources/.

attribution_lag_disclosure.narrative: Confirmed deaths can trail the national rollup by 1-3 weeks while the INRB clinical review queue catches up; for the current cycle, confirmed-case and confirmed-death zone attribution comes from the reviewed INSP SitRep Table 1. The explicit unventilated residual is disclosed and not allocated to named health zones.

insp_per_zone_block.source_review_note: Primary source-load is reviewed INSP SitRep #053 Table 1. The INRB-UMIE derived build is retained as source-review evidence where it reconciles; its confirmed-deaths table is not promoted when it fails national residual reconciliation.

upstream_reference.terms: Per-health-zone series is INSP SitRep material; reuse with attribution to INSP and citation of the report number and date; confirm distribution terms with INSP before external republication.

surveillance_zones.basis: Upstream INRB-UMIE per-zone cumulative-suspected tier, retired from the reconciled model on 2026-06-02 (national-only operational axis, never summed into confirmed). Surfaced as a surveillance signal only: the suspected figure is the upstream per-zone count on its own vintage, not a current confirmed count and not part of the national total.

response_state_block.method_basis: INRB-UMIE per-health-zone response-operations tables (contacts under follow-up, contacts seen, patients in care, hospital escapes), ND-aware latest-non-ND per zone. Province roll-ups are aggregations of the per-zone source, labelled province scope.

cross_border_land.provenance: Uganda land border closed + army-backed PoE screening (UNHCR ext-update 2026-06-04; IOM SitRep 02/03; Africa CDC SitRep 11; IMC SitRep 05)

cross_border_air.provenance: Uganda air PoE 21-day traveller screening (CDC returning-travellers info, 2026-05-21)

cross_border_ssd.provenance: South Sudan tri-border; limited/undocumented cross-border response

responseState.scope_note: Per-zone contact follow-up, care/isolation census, and hospital escapes from the INRB-UMIE per-health-zone response tables. Province figures are aggregations of the per-zone source, labelled province scope, never painted onto individual zones. A zone the source marks ND is reported as null (not reported), never zero. patients_in_care is a care/isolation census, never a case count and never a suspected case count.
responseState.method_basis: INRB-UMIE per-health-zone response-operations tables (contacts under follow-up, contacts seen, patients in care, hospital escapes), ND-aware latest-non-ND per zone. Province roll-ups are aggregations of the per-zone source, labelled province scope.

provinceCurrent.scopeNote: Province-level operational axis from the latest SitRep. INSP stopped publishing zone-level contacts/care after 2026-05-30; this is the current province and national operational state, not a per-zone value.

severity_cfr.method: Nishiura 2009 delay-adjusted confirmed CFR: confirmed deaths / sum_t(new confirmed_t * F(T - t)); F = onset-to-death gamma CDF
sources: Nishiura et al. 2009 (early-epidemic CFR bias from onset-to-death/reporting delay)
Rosello 2015 eLife onset-to-death gamma (alpha 4.42, beta 0.388/day, mean 11.4d)
Independent corroboration: Epiforecasts BVDOutbreakSize delay-corrected confirmed CFR 41.3%

estimated_total_cases.method: LOVS death-anchored range: crude death anchor (lower, deaths lag) to delay-adjusted death anchor (upper, eventual deaths), geometric-mean central; recomputed each cycle, multiplier derived

cross_check.method: Imperial College MRC GIDA, Method 2 (deaths back-projection; total symptomatic cases via CFR, a different denominator from the infection headline)

estimated_total_deaths.method: LOVS death under-ascertainment correction (range mirrors the infection bracket)

care_adjusted.method: scenario downside: if above-historical lethality (delay-adjusted cCFR over the historical BDBV CFR 95% high) is care-driven, effective IFR rises and hidden infections fall; NOT the headline and NOT a measured care correction

excess_fatality_decomposition.note: the delay-adjusted confirmed CFR relative to the historical BDBV CFR band, in death-equivalents; NOT a causal split and NOT an ascertainment measurement. beyond_historical_ci_deaths (above the 95% high) is the only portion clearly outside historical variation and is the care-SCENARIO candidate, capped consistently with the care_adjusted effective-IFR ceiling. historical_ci_band_width_deaths is the fixed width of the historical CFR 95% CI (confirmed times high minus central), a reference, not an inference about this outbreak.

death_resolution.implication: delay-adjusted lethality exceeds crude: deaths are still resolving, so the crude death anchor understates; weight the delay-adjusted upper endpoint

growth.method: trailing-window incidence growth rate: r = ln(inc2/inc1)/(window/2), floored at 0

convergence_signals.note: operational regime signals indicating which death-timing endpoint to trust; NOT burden estimates (reporting-completeness and positivity are, by design, not case-count denominators)

methodology.equation: true infections = confirmed_deaths / (death_ascertainment x IFR); implied M_stock = true infections / confirmed (derived, floats)
methodology.worked_central: crude 5820 (=582/(0.667 x 0.15)) to delay-adjusted 8493; geom-mean central 7031 (implied 4.07x on 1728)
sources: Arcede LOVS true-burden capacity model (validated 2026-06-21; death anchor + anti-correlated IFR band, now recomputed each cycle from the live death:case ratio)
Death anchor: deaths are the well-ascertained binding signal; positivity fell as testing rose (a widening net on a plateau, not hidden growth)

methodology.quantity: Estimated total infections (Imperial Method 2, external cross-check)
methodology.attribution: Imperial College MRC GIDA (external method, shown as an independent validator)
methodology.equation: C = (D / CFR) * (1 + r/beta)^alpha,   r = ln(2) / doubling
sources: Imperial College MRC GIDA, Method 2 (deaths back-projection)

methodology.equation: ascertainment = confirmed cases / estimated total infections

sources: LOVS: deaths at least as well ascertained as cases, up to near-complete

methodology.equation: under-ascertainment = estimated total infections - confirmed cases
sources: LOVS: tracked across snapshots as the surveillance-response signal

methodology.equation: floor = contacts under follow-up * per-contact secondary attack rate (adds to confirmed)
sources: LOVS Module D; Mulongo 2025 BMC (3.7% spine); Dean 2016 / Bower 2016 exposure bounds

methodology.quantity: Delay-adjusted confirmed CFR (eventual lethality among confirmed)
methodology.equation: cCFR_adj = confirmed deaths / sum_t [new confirmed_t * F(T - t)],  F = onset-to-death gamma CDF (Nishiura 2009 delay adjustment)
methodology.result: 49.2% delay-adjusted (band 45.7-53.5% over a 9-14d onset-to-death mean); crude 33.7%
sources: Nishiura et al. 2009 (early-epidemic CFR bias from onset-to-death/reporting delay)
Rosello 2015 eLife onset-to-death gamma (alpha 4.42, beta 0.388/day, mean 11.4d)
Independent corroboration: Epiforecasts BVDOutbreakSize delay-corrected confirmed CFR 41.3%
